title: trollius pumilus var. tehkehensis
intro: Trollius pumilus var. tehkehensis, also known as the dwarf globeflower, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Trollius pumilus plant, belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Trollius pumilus var. tehkehensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
